mapping.a_element: two competing behavioural strategies (spite vs. fairness) under mutation-selection
mapping.b_element: the two prey types under frequency-dependent reproduction/selection
the two pay-off matrices (prey reproduction and prey-predator interaction)

mapping.a_element: replicator-type evolutionary dynamics of strategy frequencies
mapping.b_element: replicator dynamics with predator-dependent fitnesses for the prey

mapping.a_element: self-renewable resource whose state is depleted/replenished by game outcomes (game-environment feedback)
mapping.b_element: the coupled ecological variable (logistically-limited prey / predator population) whose abundance modulates and is modulated by prey fitness

mapping.a_element: resource growth rate as the control parameter tuning the outcome
mapping.b_element: the carrying-capacity-regulating parameter as the control parameter

mapping.a_element: spite<->fairness feedback loop and resource-state transitions as the parameter varies
mapping.b_element: cascade of bifurcations, coexistence equilibria, and stable limit cycles as the parameter varies

brief_justification: Both are eco-evolutionary game dynamics: payoff-matrix-driven replicator/evolutionary selection of frequency-dependent strategies (spite/fairness vs. prey types) coupled bidirectionally to an ecological resource variable, with a single growth/carrying-capacity control parameter that tunes the system between qualitatively distinct regimes. The role-for-role mapping holds despite A being a stochastic ultimatum game with an abiotic self-renewing resource and B a deterministic Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model analysed for equilibria and bifurcations.
